Alternatively, maybe working the same duration but shifting it slightly could help her avoid rush hour traffic or areas where competition is too high during certain times. For example, if everyone starts at 8 AM, she might get caught in traffic and have lower efficiency.

But without specific information about peak hours in her area, I'll make an educated guess that starting earlier to capture more orders before the rush could be beneficial. Alternatively, if she works longer hours into the evening, say ending at 7 PM, she can handle more dinner deliveries when demand is higher. However, this depends on whether she's already working until 6 PM and needs an extra hour in the evening.

Considering her current end time is 6 PM, perhaps extending a bit later would help capture those peak evening orders. But I need to make sure that her new schedule allows her to maximize the number of orders without overlapping too much with other riders who might have similar strategies.
